title: Rhetorical Fallacies — Deep Dive
description: Beyond ad hominem: equivocation, slippery slope, motte-and-bailey, no true Scotsman.
summary_pt_br: Falácias avançadas: equivocação, slippery slope, motte-and-bailey, no true Scotsman.

scenes.subtitle_en: Beyond ad hominem: equivocation, slippery slope, motte-and-bailey, no true Scotsman.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Falácias avançadas: equivocação, slippery slope, motte-and-bailey, no true Scotsman.

scenes.text_en: Beyond ad hominem: equivocation, slippery slope, motte-and-bailey, no true Scotsman. Focus: rhetorical fallacies — deep dive. Equivocation hides behind a word changing meaning mid-argument.
scenes.text_pt: Falácias avançadas: equivocação, slippery slope, motte-and-bailey, no true Scotsman. Foco: rhetorical fallacies — deep dive. Equivocação esconde uma palavra mudando de sentido.

scenes.title: Rhetorical Fallacies — Deep Dive
scenes.body_en: Deeper fallacies: EQUIVOCATION (word shifts meaning mid-argument). SLIPPERY SLOPE (A leads inevitably to Z). MOTTE-AND-BAILEY (retreat to safe claim when challenged). NO TRUE SCOTSMAN (redefining to exclude counterexamples).
scenes.body_pt: Falácias avançadas: equivocação, slippery slope, motte-and-bailey, no true Scotsman.

examples.en: Equivocation hides behind a word changing meaning mid-argument.
examples.pt: Equivocação esconde uma palavra mudando de sentido.

examples.en: Slippery slope claims disaster without evidence.
examples.pt: Slippery slope afirma desastre sem evidência.

examples.en: No true Scotsman redefines to exclude counterexamples.
examples.pt: No true Scotsman redefine para excluir exceções.

scenes.title: Key vocabulary — Rhetorical Fallacies — Deep Dive

exercise.question: Complete: "___ hides behind a word changing meaning mid-argument."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "Equivocation hides behind a word changing meaning mid-argument."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Equivocação esconde uma palavra mudando de sentido.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
Slippery slope claims disaster without evidence.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "Slippery slope claims disaster without evidence."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Slippery slope afirma desastre sem evidência.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Rhetorical Fallacies — Deep Dive — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre rhetorical fallacies — deep dive. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Spot equivocation: 'Laws restrict freedom, so freedom requires lawlessness.'

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Bold claim, then hide behind a tamer one.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: No true Scotsman — what's it do?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Redefines group to exclude the bad example.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: To read bad faith before being persuaded.

scenes.title: The debate training
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize four advanced fallacies. Use at least three fallacy names.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: At Saturday's debate training, Zara played recordings of real political interviews to teach fallacy detection.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Equivocation hides inside a single word — watch what happens to 'freedom' across this three-sentence argument.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: And slippery slope claims that A will lead inevitably to Z without evidence for any step along the way.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Motte-and-bailey is devastating — a bold claim retreats into a cautious one the moment anyone challenges it seriously.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: No true Scotsman redefines a group to exclude every counterexample that a careful critic actually presents.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Rhetorical self-defense, Zara taught, is the grammar of reading the world without letting the world rewrite you.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Rhetorical Fallacies — Deep Dive". Beyond ad hominem: equivocation, slippery slope, motte-and-bailey, no true Scotsman. Equivocation hides behind a word changing meaning mid-argument. Slippery slope claims disaster without evidence. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Rhetorical Fallacies — Deep Dive". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
check.explanation: She says the title: "Rhetorical Fallacies — Deep Dive".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Rhetorical Fallacies — Deep Dive".

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
